The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. They need to calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be approached using combinatorial functions. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from 52 is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is $ \binom{36}{5} $ (since there are 36 non-heart cards). The number of ways to have at least one heart is the total number of 5-card hands minus the number of 5-card hands with no hearts, which is $ \binom{52}{5} - \binom{36}{5} $. Therefore, the probability is:

$$
\frac{\binom{52}{5} - \binom{36}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}}
$$

This expression represents the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt, expressed in terms of combinatorial functions.

*** 

The author has two strategies to consider. Strategy A has an expected value of $ 0.4 \times 1000 - 0.6 \times 500 = 400 - 300 = 100 $. Strategy B has an expected value of $ 0.6 \times 800 - 0.4 \times 200 = 480 - 80 = 400 $. Since the expected value of Strategy B is higher, the author should choose Strategy B.
